title: The Seven Days
thesis: Before the drama of a fallen world begins, God speaks a formless void into an ordered, good, and habitable home; this is act one of the whole story.
verse: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.

title: Your Steadfast Love, O Lord
thesis: The self-deceived wickedness that has no fear of God before its eyes stands utterly overmatched by the LORD's loving devotion, which reaches to the heavens and holds the very fountain of life and light for all who take refuge in Him.
verse: For with You is the fountain of life; in Your light we see light.

title: The Heavens Declare God's Glory
thesis: The heavens' wordless witness to God's glory and the Law's soul reviving perfection together expose the psalmist's own hidden faults, driving him to pray that his words and heart would be pleasing to the LORD, his Rock and Redeemer.
verse: The heavens declare the glory of God; the skies proclaim the work of His hands.

title: Israel Forsakes God
thesis: The LORD recalls Israel's bridal devotion in the wilderness, indicts her for forsaking Him, the fountain of living water, for broken cisterns of her own making, and exposes the folly of a people who deny their guilt even as their own history condemns them.
verse:
“For My people have committed two evils: 
They have forsaken Me, the fountain of living water, and they have dug their own cisterns, broken cisterns that cannot hold water.

title: The New Jerusalem
thesis: The bride, the holy city, descends in full splendor, gathering up temple, garden, and city imagery at once: no temple, because God and the Lamb are its temple; no curse, because the tree of life stands open again; and no night, because the Lord God is its everlasting light.
verse: Then the angel showed me a river of the water of life, as clear as crystal, flowing from the throne of God and of the Lamb

title: The Prophet's Grief and Hope
thesis: In the darkest personal suffering under God's hand, the sufferer deliberately calls to mind the steadfast love and unfailing mercies of the LORD, and on that ground he counsels patient waiting, calls the people to repentance, and entrusts his cause to the God who draws near and says, Do not be afraid.
verse: Because of the loving devotion of the LORD we are not consumed, for His mercies never fail. They are new every morning; great is Your faithfulness!

title: Bless the Lord, O My Soul
thesis: Because the LORD forgives all iniquity, heals every disease, and shows compassion as high as the heavens and as far as the east is from the west, the whole soul, and indeed all creation, is summoned to bless His holy name and never forget His kind deeds.
verse: The LORD is compassionate and gracious, slow to anger, abounding in loving devotion.

title: Teach Me Your Way, O Lord
thesis: David, poor and needy yet trusting, appeals to the LORD's kindness, forgiveness, and steadfast love, asking to be taught God's way and given an undivided heart, a prayer answered in Christ, who both perfectly walked in the Father's way and unites divided hearts to Himself.
verse: But You, O Lord, are a compassionate and gracious God, slow to anger, abounding in loving devotion and faithfulness.

title: Restoration and Rejoicing
thesis: Beyond the Day of wrath, God purifies the peoples, gathers a humble and truthful remnant with no more fear or shame, and comes as King into their midst, a mighty Savior who quiets them with His love and rejoices over them with singing.
verse: The LORD your God is among you; He is mighty to save. He will rejoice over you with gladness; He will quiet you with His love; He will rejoice over you with singing.”

title: Mourning Turned to Dancing
thesis: David's testimony that weeping may stay the night but joy comes in the morning rests on the LORD's own character, whose anger is fleeting but whose favor lasts a lifetime, turning mourning into dancing and sackcloth into gladness.
verse: For His anger is fleeting, but His favor lasts a lifetime. Weeping may stay the night, but joy comes in the morning.

title: Trust in the LORD
thesis: A wise life is not built on self reliant understanding but on whole hearted trust in the LORD, a trust that shapes worship, welcomes discipline, prizes wisdom above riches, and works itself out in concrete kindness toward others.
verse: Trust in the LORD with all your heart, and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways acknowledge Him, and He will make your paths straight.

title: The Lord Is My Shepherd
thesis: David's confidence that the LORD is his shepherd, providing, guiding, and protecting him even through the valley of the shadow of death, is fulfilled completely in Jesus Christ, the good shepherd who lays down His life for His sheep.
verse: The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.

title: God Is Our Refuge and Strength
thesis: Though the earth shakes and nations rage, God's own presence with His people, pictured as a river gladdening the city of God, is unshakable refuge and strength, calling every anxious heart to be still and know that He is God, a peace fulfilled in Christ, Immanuel.
verse: God is our refuge and strength, an ever-present help in times of trouble.

title: The Helper of Israel
thesis: The LORD who alone raises up and casts down kings, who alone declared the future before it happened, is the same God who takes Israel's trembling, worm-like weakness by the hand and promises, again and again, do not fear, I will help you.
verse: Do not fear, for I am with you; do not be afraid, for I am your God. I will strengthen you; I will surely help you; I will uphold you with My right hand of righteousness.

title: The Sermon on the Mount: Prayer and Treasure
thesis: True righteousness is lived before the Father who sees in secret, not staged for human applause, and it flows from a heart set on God alone, so that His people pray to their Father, treasure heaven above earth, serve God rather than money, and are freed from anxiety to seek first His kingdom.
verse: But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness, and all these things will be added unto you.

title: Rejoice and Be at Peace
thesis: Paul urges two coworkers to be reconciled, then calls the whole church to rejoice in the Lord always, to trade anxiety for thankful prayer so that the peace of God guards their hearts and minds in Christ, and to fix their thoughts on what is true and honorable, promising that the God of peace will be with them.
verse: Be anxious for nothing, but in everything, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God. And the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.

title: Shepherd the Flock
thesis: Under the Chief Shepherd who will appear with an unfading crown, elders shepherd God's flock willingly and humbly, all clothe themselves in humility and cast their anxieties on the God who cares for them, and resisting the devil they trust that the God of all grace will Himself restore and establish them after a little suffering.
verse: Cast all your anxiety on Him, because He cares for you.

title: The Word Became Flesh
thesis: The eternal Word who was God and was with God became flesh to bring life and light into a dark world, and though His own did not receive Him, to all who believe in His name He gives the right to become children of God, and through Him the unseen God is at last made known.
verse: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.

title: Nicodemus and the New Birth
thesis: Entry into the kingdom of God is not by human descent, effort, or learning but by a birth from above, worked by the Spirit, and grounded in the Son of Man lifted up like the serpent in the wilderness, so that everyone who believes in the Son whom God gave in love will not perish but have eternal life.
verse: For God so loved the world that He gave His one and only Son, that everyone who believes in Him shall not perish but have eternal life.

title: The Way, the Truth, and the Life
thesis: Jesus calms His disciples' fear by promising to prepare a place for them and to come again, revealing Himself as the only way to the Father and the perfect image of the Father, and pledging the gift of the Spirit and His own peace, so that His departure is not abandonment but the path to a deeper union with the Father and the Son.
verse: Jesus answered, “I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through Me.

title: To Us a Child Is Born
thesis: Where the previous chapter ended in darkness, God answers with the promise of a child, born for us, given to us, whose divine titles and everlasting, just reign will finally undo every yoke of oppression, guaranteed by the zeal of the LORD of Hosts Himself.
verse:
For unto us a child is born, unto us a son is given, 
and the government will be upon His shoulders. And He will be called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, 
Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.

title: The Suffering Servant
thesis: The Servant of the LORD bears, in His own body and by God's own will, the guilt, punishment, and sickness that belonged to His people, dying an unjust death as a guilt offering for the many, and is afterward raised to see His offspring, prolong His days, and divide the spoils, because His substitutionary suffering has already succeeded.
verse: But He was pierced for our transgressions, He was crushed for our iniquities; the punishment that brought us peace was upon Him, and by His stripes we are healed.

title: The Supremacy of Christ
thesis: The Son is supreme in every sphere, over all creation as its maker and over the new creation as the risen head of the church, for in Him all the fullness of God was pleased to dwell and through His blood God has reconciled all things, so that the Colossians, once hostile, now stand reconciled if they continue in the gospel.
verse: For in Him all things were created, things in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible, whether thrones or dominions or rulers or authorities. All things were created through Him and for Him. He is before all things, and in Him all things hold together.

title: God Has Spoken by His Son
thesis: God's revelation reaches its climax in the Son, who is heir of all things, the agent of creation, the radiance of God's glory and the exact imprint of His nature, the sustainer of all things, and the one who, having purified sins, sat down at God's right hand.
verse: The Son is the radiance of God’s glory and the exact representation of His nature, upholding all things by His powerful word. After He had provided purification for sins, He sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high.

title: An Invitation to the Thirsty
thesis: Because the Servant's suffering has secured salvation, God now issues an open, urgent invitation to everyone who thirsts to come without money and be satisfied, to seek Him while He may be found, and to trust that His word, once sent out, will not return empty but will accomplish everything He intends.
verse: “Come, all you who are thirsty, come to the waters; and you without money, come, buy, and eat! Come, buy wine and milk without money and without cost!

title: The Samaritan Woman and the Official's Son
thesis: Jesus freely crosses the barriers of race, gender, and shame to give living water that becomes a spring of eternal life, revealing Himself as the Christ and the Savior of the world whom the Father seeks worshipers to worship in spirit and truth, and He is trusted at His bare word by an official whose son He heals from afar.
verse: But whoever drinks the water I give him will never thirst. Indeed, the water I give him will become in him a fount of water springing up to eternal life.”

title: At the Feast of Tabernacles
thesis: At the Feast of Tabernacles Jesus teaches with an authority that is from the Father and not His own, and on the last great day He offers the living water of the Spirit to all who thirst and believe, so that the feast becomes the setting for a decisive division over whether He is the Christ.
verse: On the last and greatest day of the feast, Jesus stood up and called out in a loud voice, “If anyone is thirsty, let him come to Me and drink. Whoever believes in Me, as the Scripture has said: ‘Streams of living water will flow from within him.’”

title: John's Question and Come to Me
thesis: Jesus answers doubt with the evidence of His messianic works, exposes the hardness of a generation that will receive neither John nor Him, warns the privileged but unrepentant of judgment, and reveals Himself as the Son who alone knows the Father and who welcomes the weary with the offer of rest under His gentle yoke.
verse: Come to Me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.

title: The Light of the World and Before Abraham
thesis: Jesus is the light of the world and the great I am, sent from the Father, whose truth alone sets people free from slavery to sin, so that the deepest issue is not descent from Abraham but whether one belongs to God and believes the Son who existed before Abraham was born.
verse: Once again, Jesus spoke to the people and said, “I am the light of the world. Whoever follows Me will never walk in the darkness, but will have the light of life.”

title: Delight in the Law of the Lord
thesis: The psalmist's entire life, praise and obedience, affliction and tears, hope and final confession, revolves around delight in God's word, a delight that only makes full sense fulfilled in Christ, the Word made flesh, who perfectly loved and kept the law and now writes that same word on His people's hearts.
verse: Your word is a lamp to my feet and a light to my path.

title: Comfort for God's People
thesis: To a people who feel forgotten in exile, God speaks comfort by declaring their sin pardoned, sending a herald to prepare His way, and revealing Himself as the incomparable Creator who measures the oceans in His palm and yet gently carries His lambs, so that those who wait on Him will find their strength renewed.
verse: The grass withers and the flowers fall, but the word of our God stands forever.”

title: The Promised Rest
thesis: The rest God promised was not exhausted by Canaan or by Joshua, for God still says 'today'; a Sabbath rest remains for the people of God, entered by faith and not by unbelief, and the living, penetrating word of God lays every heart bare before the One to whom we must give account.
verse: For the word of God is living and active. Sharper than any double-edged sword, it pierces even to dividing soul and spirit, joints and marrow. It judges the thoughts and intentions of the heart.

title: Continue in the Scriptures
thesis: In contrast to deceivers who go from bad to worse, Timothy must continue in the Scriptures he has known from infancy, which make him wise for salvation through faith in Christ, for all Scripture is God-breathed and profitable to equip the man of God completely for every good work.
verse: All Scripture is God-breathed and is useful for instruction, for conviction, for correction, and for training in righteousness,

title: Be Strong and Courageous
thesis: The land is God's gift and God's oath, not Joshua's achievement; so the courage Joshua is commanded to have rests not on his own strength but on the presence of the LORD who never leaves him and on the word of the LORD he is to meditate on day and night.
verse: This Book of the Law must not depart from your mouth; meditate on it day and night, so that you may be careful to do everything written in it. For then you will prosper and succeed in all you do.

title: Comfort for God's People
thesis: To a people who feel forgotten in exile, God speaks comfort by declaring their sin pardoned, sending a herald to prepare His way, and revealing Himself as the incomparable Creator who measures the oceans in His palm and yet gently carries His lambs, so that those who wait on Him will find their strength renewed.
verse: But those who wait upon the LORD will renew their strength; they will mount up with wings like eagles; they will run and not grow weary, they will walk and not faint.

title: Be Strong and Courageous
thesis: The land is God's gift and God's oath, not Joshua's achievement; so the courage Joshua is commanded to have rests not on his own strength but on the presence of the LORD who never leaves him and on the word of the LORD he is to meditate on day and night.
verse: Have I not commanded you to be strong and courageous? Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged, for the LORD your God is with you wherever you go.”

title: Contentment and Final Greetings
thesis: Paul rejoices in the Philippians' renewed generosity, yet grounds his joy not in the gift but in a Christ-given contentment learned through plenty and want, so that he can do all things through Christ who strengthens him; and he assures them that their gift, a fragrant offering to God, will be answered as God supplies all their needs from His riches in Christ, to whom be glory forever.
verse: I can do all things through Christ who gives me strength.

title: The Lord Is My Light and My Salvation
thesis: David's confidence that the LORD is his light and salvation does not remove his fear and need, but drives him to seek God's face all the more urgently, resting his hope on the certainty that he will see God's goodness in the land of the living.
verse: The LORD is my light and my salvation, whom shall I fear? The LORD is the stronghold of my life, whom shall I dread?

title: Paul's Vision and Thorn
thesis: The gospel minister's true credential is not spiritual experience but the sufficiency of Christ's grace displayed in weakness, for the Lord perfects His power in weakness, and the servant who is weak in himself is strong in Christ.
verse: But He said to me, “My grace is sufficient for you, for My power is perfected in weakness.” Therefore I will boast all the more gladly in my weaknesses, so that the power of Christ may rest on me.

title: My Help Comes from the Lord
thesis: The LORD who made heaven and earth is an ever watchful keeper who never slumbers, sheltering His people from every danger by day and night, from this moment until forevermore.
verse: I lift up my eyes to the hills. From where does my help come? My help comes from the LORD, the Maker of heaven and earth.

title: Wisdom and Folly
thesis: Two women call from their houses to the same simple passers by, Wisdom, offering a feast that leads to life and understanding, and Folly, offering stolen pleasure that leads to death, and every person must choose which table to sit at.
verse: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom, and knowledge of the Holy One is understanding.

title: The LORD's Lawsuit and What He Requires
thesis: The God who redeemed His people has a righteous case against their ingratitude and injustice; He does not want mountains of sacrifice but justice, mercy, and a humble walk with Himself, and where these are refused He justly sentences a corrupt society to futility.
verse: He has shown you, O man, what is good. And what does the LORD require of you but to act justly, to love mercy, and to walk humbly with your God?

title: A Time for Everything
thesis: God has fixed a time for everything and does all things well, yet He hides the whole from us and sets eternity in our hearts, so that we cannot master life but are driven to fear Him and to receive daily bread, work, and joy as His gift.
verse: To everything there is a season, and a time for every purpose under heaven:

title: Living Sacrifices
thesis: Because of God's mercies, the whole of the redeemed life is to be laid on the altar as a living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to God; and this worship works itself out in a renewed mind, a humble self-estimate, and the loving use of every gift within the one body of Christ.
verse: Do not be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind. Then you will be able to test and approve what is the good, pleasing, and perfect will of God.

title: The Sermon on the Mount: Beatitudes and the Law
thesis: Jesus unveils the life of the kingdom: a blessedness that belongs to the humble and hungry, a public witness of salt and light, and a righteousness that, far from abolishing the Law, fulfills it by reaching the heart, so that His people are called to a wholeness of love that mirrors their heavenly Father.
verse: In the same way, let your light shine before men, that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father in heaven.

title: By Grace Through Faith
thesis: We were spiritually dead, enslaved, and under wrath, but God, rich in mercy and great in love, made us alive with Christ, raised us, and seated us with Him, saving us by grace through faith as His own gift, so that no one can boast, and creating us anew in Christ for the good works He prepared beforehand.
verse: For it is by grace you have been saved through faith, and this not from yourselves; it is the gift of God, not by works, so that no one can boast.

title: Future Glory
thesis: The sufferings of this present time cannot be compared with the glory to be revealed; creation and believers groan in hope while the Spirit intercedes, and God works all things together for the good of those He foreknew, predestined, called, justified, and will surely glorify.
verse: And we know that God works all things together for the good of those who love Him, who are called according to His purpose.

title: Accepting One Another
thesis: The strong ought to bear with the weak and please their neighbor for his upbuilding, following Christ who did not please Himself; and the whole church, Jew and Gentile, is to welcome one another as Christ welcomed them, so that with one voice they glorify the God of hope who fills them with joy and peace by the power of the Holy Spirit.
verse: Now may the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace as you believe in Him, so that you may overflow with hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.

title: A New Heaven and a New Earth
thesis: The dwelling of God with man, promised since Eden and pictured in the tabernacle, is here declared complete: the old order of death, mourning, and pain passes away, all things are made new, and every reader is confronted with the choice between overcoming faith and the second death.
verse: ‘He will wipe away every tear from their eyes,’ and there will be no more death or mourning or crying or pain, for the former things have passed away.”

title: A Living Hope
thesis: Because God in His great mercy has given us new birth into a living hope and an imperishable inheritance kept safe in heaven, believers rejoice even through fiery trials that prove their faith, receiving the salvation the prophets foretold and the angels long to see.
verse: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! By His great mercy He has given us new birth into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,
